measurements.measurement_reference: Ground floor window height ~1.3m, approximately 18 courses sill to lintel

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard half-lap running bond throughout. No header courses visible. Consistent coursing across full facade width.

colour.description: Medium red-brown brick with scattered darker headers/stretchers throughout. Some bricks show deeper brown-red tones while others are lighter orange-red. Typical of mid-range Toronto residential brick from early 20th century. Not the fine uniformity of institutional brick but reasonably consistent.

special_features.description: Rowlock course forming projecting window sills on both storeys
Flat jack arches in brick above window openings, approximately 3-4 courses deep

condition.notes: 2-storey brick duplex in typical Kensington condition. Address '112' clearly visible on facade. Brick intact but some darkening/soiling on lower courses. No structural cracking visible. Gable end above second floor is clad in siding, not brick.

texture_calibration.notes: Measurement estimated from ground floor window. Photo is face-on but some perspective distortion due to wide angle. Speed limit sign and utility pole partially obscure some coursing.
